original: Four boys are the first to be rescued after 16 days, from Tham Luang cave, Thailand, by Thai and international rescue team
Alfred Carlton Gilbert's patent for the Erector Set is issued, one of the most popular toys of all time
"Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire," the 4th book in the series byJ. K. Rowling, is published in the UK (Bloomsbury) and the US (Scholastic)
"Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban," the 3rd book in the series byJ. K. Rowling, is published by Bloomsbury in the UK
Farthest thrown object - an "Aerobie" flying ring, 383 m (1,257 ft)
Reports are broadcast that a UFO has crash landed in Roswell, New Mexico
GymnastSimone Bilesaged 25, becomes the youngest person to receive the US Presidential Medal of Freedom from PresidentJoe Biden
Zaila Avant-garde, American student, age 14, from Harvey, Louisiana, wins Scripps National Spelling Bee title
Godtfred Kirk Christiansen, Danish toy manufacturer (Lego Group), born in Billund, Denmark (d. 1995)
Voyager 2 takes first ever photo of Jupiter's satellite Adrastea (J14)
Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched in the final mission of the U.S. Space Shuttle program
South Korean golfer Sei Young Ki breaks LPGA 72-hole scoring record with 31 under-par 257, also first to better -30, in winning Thornberry Creek Classic
FIFA World Cup: Germany defeat Brazil by a record 7-1 in the semi-finals to make it to the final;Miroslav Kloseof Germany breaks the World Cup goal scoring record with 16 goals
12:34:56 on 7/8/90 (1234567890), a once-in-lifetime moment
A tinyLeonardo da Vincisketch "Head of a Bear," the size of a Post-it, sells for £8.9 millioin ($12.2 million) at auction in London
Americans and Polynesians made contact around 1200 A.D. according to new genomic study in "Nature"; people from eastern Polynesia had DNA from indigenous Colombia
Portuguese navigatorVasco da Gamadeparts on his first voyage and becomes the first European to reach India by sea
Dr. Benjamin Waterhouse gives the first cowpox vaccination in the United States to his son to prevent smallpox
British girl group theSpice Girlsrelease their debut single "Wannabe" in the UK
Maya Hawke, American actress (Stranger Things), born in New York City
Riele Downs, Canadian actress (Henry Danger), born in Toronto, Ontario
Kevin Bacon, American actor (Diner; Footloose; She's Having a Baby), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Beck [Bek David Campbell], American singer-songwriter, musician and record producer ("Loser"; "Where It's At"), born in Los Angeles, California
Son Heung-min, South Korean soccer forward (Hamburger SV, Bayer Leverkusen, Tottenham Hotspur), born in Chuncheon, South Korea
CommodoreMatthew C. Perrysails his frigate Susquehanna into Tokyo Bay, opening Japan to Western influence and trade
Alfred Binet, French child psychologist who invented the first practical IQ test (Binet–Simon test), born in Nice, France (d. 1911)
Arthur Evans, English archaeologist (discovered the Minoan palace of Knossos in Crete), born in Nash Mills, England (d. 1941)
Trailing 7-0, Brewers tie Angels and then score 13 in 5th to win 20-7
Independent Vermont introduces a new constitution prohibiting slavery
Colonel John Nixon gives the first public reading of the Declaration of Independence to an assemblage of citizens in Philadelphia

John L. Sullivansuccessfully defends the last officially sanctioned bare-knuckle world heavyweight prizefighting championship when Jake Kilrain's trainer throws in the towel after 75 one-minute rounds near Hattiesburg, Mississippi
Charles Darwinreaches Saint Helena in HMS Beagle and takes up lodgings near the tomb of Napoleon
George Antheil, American pianist, avant garde composer (Airplane Sonata; Ballet Mécanique), and inventor, born in Trenton, New Jersey (d. 1959)

Benjamin Waugh and others found The London Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children, in London; later expanded to become the National Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children (NSPCC)
George Washington headquarters at West Point for his Continental Army
Space Shuttle STS-65 (Columbia 17) launches
During 1st round of the John Deere Classic, Paul Goydos becomes the 4th and oldest player in PGA Tour history to shoot 59; round included 12 birdies and 6 pars
MLB Padres relief pitcher Daniel Camarena gets his first hit, in his second career at bat - a grand slam - against Washington Nationals' Max Scherzer, in San Diego
FIFA World Cup Final, Stadio Olimpico, Rome, Italy: 85th-minute penalty by Andreas Brehme gives West Germany 1-0 win over Argentina
Japan says the Olympics will be held without spectators as Tokyo announces state of emergency due to a surge in COVID-19
The NFL's Washington Redskins have their trademark vacated on the grounds it may cause offence to native Americans
Pioneer-Venus 2 Multi-probe launched to Venus
Unesco declares Asmara, capital of Eritrea, a world heritage site for its Art Deco buildings
Frost in Waltham, Massachusetts during "year without a summer"
Walter Mittelholzerflies a Junkers F-13 to Spitsbergen, Svalbard
Earliest known date on a Maya inscription, possibly depicting the enthronement of King Siyaj Chan K'awiil I of Tikal (Stela 29 Tikal)
Dominique Jean Larrey, French surgeon under Napoleon, first modern military surgeon, born in Beaudéan, Pyrenees (d. 1842)
Francis Leopold McClintock, Irish-born British naval officer and explorer who confirmed fate of Franklin's 1845 Artic expedition, born in Dundalk, Ireland (d. 1907)
Therese of Saxe-Hildburghausen, German Queen of Bavaria as consort of King Ludwig I, Oktoberfest created for her wedding, born in Duchy of Saxe-Hildburghausen (d. 1854)
Artemisia Gentileschi, Italian baroque painter, the most famous European female painter of the 17th century, born in Rome (d. 1653)[1]
Harrison Dillard, American athlete (Olympic gold 100m, 4×100m relay 1948; 110m hurdles, 4×100m relay 1952), born in Cleveland, Ohio (d. 2019)
Jane Tehira, New Zealand sportsperson (triple international: basketball, softball, hockey), born in Kaikohe, New Zealand (d. 2023)
Millard Hampton, American athlete (Olympic gold 4x100m relay 1976), born in Fresno, California
Lisa Powell, Australian field hockey forward (Olympic gold 1996, 2000; World Cup gold 1994, 98; Champions Trophy gold x 5), born in Sydney, Australia
Valerii Zaluzhnyi, Ukrainian general (Commander-in-Chief of  Armed Forces of Ukraine during Russian invasion), born in Zviahel, Ukrainian SSR, Soviet Union
Wang Zhizhi, Chinese basketball center (China's first NBA player; Dallas Mavericks, LA Clippers, Miami Heat; CBA MVP 2000 Bayi Rockets), born in Beijing, China
Jamie Cook, British guitarist (Arctic Monkeys - "I Bet You Look Good On The Dancefloor"), born in High Green, Sheffield, England
Raffi [Cavoukian], Armenian-Canadian children's singer and lyricist ("Baby Beluga"), born in Cairo, Egypt
Zhou Long, Chinese-American composer (Madame White Snake - Pulitzer Prize for Music 2011), born in Beijing, China[1]
Devon Conway, New Zealand cricket batsman (7th batsman to score double century on Test debut; 200 v England 2021; Gauteng, Wellington, Somerset CCC), born in Johannesburg, South Africa
